topic.what: Systematic evaluation of prompt injection defenses using adaptive evolutionary attacks over extended campaigns
topic.how: Built agentic attacker that generates and evolves strategies across 500 rounds; tested 9 defense configurations across 20,000+ attacks against Gemini 2.5 Pro with black-box methodology
topic.why_matters: Prompt injection is OWASP's #1 LLM vulnerability; real-world leaks have exposed Bing Chat system prompts, Snapchat My AI rules, and 1.5M Moltbook API tokens; static evaluations overestimate defense effectiveness

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of rounds with detected secret or instruction leaks
characteristics.context: 15,000 attacks on t5/t7 with zero leaks vs 20,000+ total attacks across all defenses

characteristics.what_it_measures: Rounds required to reach 0.9+ severity score
characteristics.context: All model-reliant defenses broke within 300 rounds; instruction hierarchy held longest at 247, sandwich at 277

characteristics.what_it_measures: Maximum leak score (0.00-1.00) achieved per defense
characteristics.context: Ablation t8 (without output filter) limited severity to 0.10 vs 0.9+ for single model-reliant defenses

characteristics.what_it_measures: Rounds to reach peak severity on no-defense baseline across models
characteristics.vs_baseline: All models eventually leaked all secrets
characteristics.context: Gemini 2.5 Pro, GPT-5.4, Claude Sonnet 4.6 with default safety settings all compromised

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of attacks achieving leak scores above zero
characteristics.value: 0% for output filtering defenses across 15,000 attacks
characteristics.vs_baseline: Model-reliant defenses achieved significant leak rates under sustained pressure
characteristics.context: 3 independent 500-round runs each for t5 and t7 totaling 15,000 attacks with zero leaks

characteristics.what_it_measures: Consistency of defense effectiveness over extended campaign duration
characteristics.vs_baseline: Short 25-round evaluations misleading for all defenses
characteristics.evidence: Two-phase probing-then-breakthrough pattern in score progression
characteristics.context: Sandwich defense: 0.4% leak rate at 25 rounds -> 3.8% at 277 rounds; severity 0.05 -> 0.95

good_for: Production LLM applications with embedded secrets
Security audit and red teaming workflows
not_for: Indirect prompt injection via retrieved data
requires: Access to LLM API for defender/attacker setup
Deterministic substring matching for output filtering
Extended campaign runs (500+ rounds) for durability testing

novelty: First evaluation of output filtering under adaptive pressure across 15,000 attacks
Agentic evolutionary attacker with 40+ strategies and 500-round campaigns
Cross-model comparison with default safety settings on Gemini 2.5 Pro, GPT-5.4, Claude Sonnet 4.6
Two-phase probing-then-breakthrough pattern discovery in defense decay
Ablation study isolating output filtering contribution in multi-layer defense
open_problems: Extending cross-model testing to all 9 defense configurations
Fuzzy leak detection for paraphrased/encoded secret extraction
Architectural defenses for browser-based prompt storage vectors
Semantic evaluator layering without creating new attack surface
